methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy

data.narrative:
Calogena is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Frédéric Mariotte that has raised €100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 entities: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers in the nuclear energy sector, including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. A sufficient narrow peer set meeting stage-specific and tier-specific filters could not be constructed; only 1 same-stage comparable and 1 adjacent-stage comparable were identified within the nuclear energy sector, falling short of the minimum 3 datapoints required for robust median analysis. While 8 private nuclear peers exist broadly, filtering by valuation tier yielded insufficient matches. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the capital intensity and stage-specific dynamics of nuclear ventures.

This valuation exercise is constrained by the limited depth of comparables at Calogena's specific stage and capital tier within nuclear energy. A defensible range cannot be triangulated from the available data. Meaningful analysis would require either expansion of the comp set to include adjacent deep-tech hardware stages, access to transaction multiples from recent nuclear financings, or forward-looking metrics specific to Calogena's project timeline and technology maturity. The key uncertainty is whether precedent from earlier-stage or later-stage nuclear peers appropriately reflects Series A risk and capital requirements in this sector.
